title: Rainbow Snake Surprise (JOE-7)
content:
## Overview JOE-7 adds a rare easter egg: on roughly 1 in 100 games, player 1's snake renders as an animated rainbow and a celebratory banner appears. The effect must not alter normal gameplay, must persist for the entire game session once activated, and must be visibly animated (not gated behind `prefers-reduced-motion`). Rainbow games also route scores to a hidden leaderboard that becomes visible only after a player earns their first rainbow score. ## Files Changed - `src/main.ts` — roll logic, state field, URL seam, `draw()` per-segment coloring, banner show/hide methods, `leaderboardTargetMode()` helper, `updateRainbowTabVisibility()` - `src/leaderboard.ts` — `'rainbow'` mode type and display label - `index.html` — `#rainbow-banner` element, hidden rainbow leaderboard tab - `src/style.css` — `.rainbow-banner` styles and keyframe animations, rainbow tab styles ## Per-Game Roll A constant `RAINBOW_CHANCE = 0.01` is defined near the other tuning constants. The `SnakeGame` class holds a private `rainbowSnake: boolean` field. In `startNewGame()`, after the state reset block, the field is set: ```ts this.rainbowSnake = this.rainbowOverride ?? (Math.random() < RAINBOW_CHANCE); ``` ### URL Override Seam Because a 1-in-100 event cannot be reliably exercised in automated tests, a `?rainbow=1` or `?rainbow=0` query parameter overrides the roll deterministically. The override is parsed once in the constructor into `private rainbowOverride: boolean | null`. This has no effect in production. ## Rainbow Rendering Per-segment rainbow coloring is implemented in the `draw()` method. Each player-1 snake segment is assigned an HSL hue derived from its position along the body: ``` h = ((index / len) * 360 + phase) % 360 ``` The `phase` value scrolls over time using `performance.now()`, producing an animated shimmer. The existing per-frame canvas redraw (driven by the board auto-rotation rAF loop) animates the effect for free — no additional animation loop is needed. A helper `rainbowFaces(index, len, phase)` returns three HSL face shades matching the existing isometric shading model (top lightest → left darkest): - Top: `hsl(h, 90%, 62%)` - Left: `hsl(h, 90%, 50%)` - Right: `hsl(h, 90%, 40%)` HSL strings are valid `ctx.fillStyle` values directly, so no hex conversion is needed (unlike the existing `lerpHex` helper, which is hex-only). **Scope:** Player 1's snake only. In two-player modes, player 2's snake remains blue so player identity is preserved. **Hunger tint interaction:** Rainbow coloring supersedes the green→warning `tint()` blend for body colors during a rainbow game. The starvation cue is not lost — it is still carried by the pulsing starvation text banner (`updateStarvationWarning`). ## "FRIENDSHIP!"-Style Banner The banner is styled after the classic Mortal Kombat friendship finisher. Text reads **"Rainbow Snake"** in chunky all-caps arcade lettering. ### HTML (`index.html`) Inside `.canvas-wrap`, alongside `#death-banner`: ```html
Rainbow Snake
``` ### CSS (`src/style.css`) - `.rainbow-banner` — absolute-positioned, centered near the top of the board, `pointer-events: none`. Reveals with a **bounce pop-in** (`friendship-pop` keyframe: scale from 0.5→1 with a cubic-bezier overshoot). Dismisses with a CSS opacity fade. - `.rainbow-arc` — a semicircular rainbow rendered via a bottom-anchored `radial-gradient` clipped to the top half, with concentric bands of red→orange→yellow→green→blue→indigo→violet sitting behind the text. - `.rainbow-text` — `text-transform: uppercase`, `font-weight: 900`, generous `letter-spacing`, bright yellow (`#ffe600`) fill with a thick dark outline built from layered `text-shadow`s. Flickers three times via a `friendship-flash` blink animation (`steps(2), 3 iterations`) before settling. ### Logic (`src/main.ts`) Modelled on the existing `showDeathBanner`/`hideDeathBanner` pattern: - `showRainbowBanner()` — removes `hidden`, then schedules `hideRainbowBanner()` via `window.setTimeout` after 3000 ms (timer ID stored in `rainbowBannerTimer`). - `hideRainbowBanner()` — adds `hidden` and calls `clearTimeout` on the stored timer. - Called from `startNewGame()` at the end, once the game screen is shown. - Also called in reset, game-over, and back-to-menu paths via `showScreen` so a stale banner never lingers. ## Hidden Rainbow Leaderboard ### Behavior A secret fourth leaderboard collects scores exclusively from rainbow games. The tab starts `hidden` in `index.html` and becomes visible only after the board contains at least one entry. Clearing the rainbow board re-hides the tab automatically. There is no separate unlock flag — visibility is derived purely from whether the board has entries, keeping the reveal stateless and self-healing. Rainbow scores are **never cross-posted** to the normal single-player or PvP/BvB leaderboards. The secret board is a distinct prize, not a duplicate of existing standings. After a rainbow run ends, the "View Leaderboard" button opens directly to the newly revealed rainbow board. ### Score Routing (`src/main.ts`) A `leaderboardTargetMode()` helper redirects any run flagged as a rainbow game to the secret board instead of the normal mode board. All three scoring paths — single-player prompt, PvP prompt, and both auto-recorded Bot vs Bot entries — go through this helper, ensuring rainbow scores never appear on standard boards. ### Tab Visibility (`src/main.ts`) `updateRainbowTabVisibility()` shows or hides the rainbow tab based on whether the board holds any entries. It is called every time the leaderboard screen opens. ### Storage and Rendering (`src/leaderboard.ts`) `'rainbow'` is added to the leaderboard mode type with the display label `🌈 Rainbow Snake`. The board reuses the existing storage and ranking machinery without new persistence logic, keying off `snake-leaderboard-v1:rainbow` automatically. Because the rainbow board aggregates runs from all game modes, its Result and Opponent columns render based on what each entry actually carries rather than a fixed mode assumption. This avoids showing empty columns for single-player entries or hiding result columns for PvP entries on the same board. ## Testing A `?rainbow=1/0` URL parameter forces the outcome for Playwright tests and manual verification. The recommended E2E assertions are: - `?rainbow=1`: `#rainbow-banner` is visible with text "Rainbow Snake", then auto-hides after ~3 s. - `?rainbow=0`: banner remains hidden throughout. - Two-player mode with `?rainbow=1`: player 2's snake stays blue. - `?rainbow=1` after a completed game: rainbow leaderboard tab becomes visible; score does not appear on the standard board. Canvas pixel colors are not asserted — the DOM banner, forced flag, and leaderboard tab visibility are the reliable observables.
